name: Professor Irwin Corey
blurb: Professor Irwin Corey was an American stand-up comic and actor billed as The World's Foremost Authority, performing a wild-haired, double-talking mock-professor character whose elaborate non-sequitur monologues parodied.
bio: Professor Irwin Corey was an American stand-up comic and actor billed as The World's Foremost Authority, performing a wild-haired, double-talking mock-professor character whose elaborate non-sequitur monologues parodied academic jargon. He introduced his improvisational style at the San Francisco club the hungry i and performed for more than seventy years. Lenny Bruce called him one of the most brilliant comedians of all time.

timeline.ev: Returned to NYC; wrote and performed in revue Pins and Needles
Develops the World's Foremost Authority character at the hungry i in San Francisco
Appeared in New Faces of 1943 and at the Village Vanguard
Played Abou Ben Atom in the Broadway musical Flahooley
Accepts the National Book Award for Thomas Pynchon's Gravity's Rainbow on the author's behalf
Accepted Thomas Pynchon's National Book Award for Gravity's Rainbow
Appeared as 'The Mad Bomber' in the film Car Wash
Dies at age 102 in New York City
Died February 6 in Manhattan at age 102

accolades: Billed for over 70 years as The World's Foremost Authority
Praised by Lenny Bruce as one of the most brilliant comedians of all time
Billed as 'The World's Foremost Authority'; Lenny Bruce called him 'one of the most brilliant comedians of all time'
Chosen by Thomas Pynchon to accept the 1974 National Book Award for Gravity's Rainbow on his behalf

movies_tv.t: The Curse of the Jade Scorpion

machine.reason: zero clips on file — every comic needs at least 1 official clip

flags.bits_honest_gap_note: No embeddable OFFICIAL stand-up footage found. Corey (d.2017, 'World's Foremost Authority') has plentiful YouTube footage but every located clip is a fan/archivist re-upload, not an official channel or rights-holder: Smothers Brothers clips are 2008 user uploads; the Dec 6 1983 Letterman appearance (-CsdRGbQPr0) is on archivist 'Don Giller' (NOT the official Letterman channel); 'Laugh Tracks Legends of Comedy' (p0wGfyRSuYM) is a fan compilation; Mike Douglas/misc clips likewise. Searches of the official 'The Ed Sullivan Show' channel (@TheEdSullivanShow, 3,500+ archive videos) surfaced no Corey clip. An 'Irwin Corey - Topic' channel exists (UCVuYvp2ZJ8eYW4q-oUgSj0g) but its Videos and Releases tabs are EMPTY ('doesn't have any') — no embeddable album-audio track to verify, despite real LPs ('Win With Irwin' 1960, 'The World's Foremost Authority?' 1967). Per the no-fan-rips rule, nothing is listed. Honest gap.
